guardrails.title: Security Impact Disclosure
guardrails.body: Changes affecting authentication, authorization, or input validation shall explicitly state their security impact in the spec.

guardrails.title: Input Sanitization Required
guardrails.body: Changes handling user input shall specify countermeasures against SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and similar attacks in the spec.

guardrails.title: No Reinventing Framework Features
guardrails.body: When the framework provides a built-in feature, custom implementations of the same functionality are prohibited.

guardrails.title: CSRF Protection Required
guardrails.body: Requests that cause state changes shall have CSRF protection. Do not disable the framework's built-in CSRF protection.

guardrails.title: No Sensitive Files in Public Directory
guardrails.body: Do not place configuration files, .env, backups, .git, or other sensitive files in the public directory (webroot).

guardrails.title: Server-Side Authorization
guardrails.body: Authorization checks shall be performed on the server side. Access control solely through hiding UI elements is prohibited.

guardrails.title: Secure Session Handling
guardrails.body: Regenerate session IDs after login. Cookies shall have HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite attributes.

guardrails.title: Use Standard Password Hashing
guardrails.body: Do not store passwords in plaintext. Custom hash implementations are prohibited. Use the standard hashing functions provided by the language or framework.

guardrails.title: No SELECT *
guardrails.body: Explicitly enumerate required columns in SELECT statements. SELECT * increases network transfer volume, causes unintended behavior changes when columns are added, and prevents covering index utilization.

guardrails.title: Cursor Pagination Over OFFSET
guardrails.body: Use cursor-based pagination instead of OFFSET for paginating large datasets. OFFSET scans an increasing number of rows as pages go deeper, degrading performance.

guardrails.title: Transaction Scope Minimization
guardrails.body: Keep the scope of transactions to the minimum necessary. Do not include external API calls, file I/O, or heavy computation inside transactions. The longer the transaction, the longer locks are held, increasing the risk of deadlocks and timeouts.
